steps.title: Match speech and information rates
steps.content: The researchers claim that languages can have very different spoken rates while conveying about the same amount of information per second. Supporting data must therefore pair a slower and faster language with similar information rates.

steps.title: Reject mismatched patterns
steps.content: A says the slowest languages also convey the least information, which does not show equal information despite different speed. C links fastest speech with fastest information. D compares similar speech rates rather than very different ones. The answer is B.
